payload.title: Activation that's invisible to stock rustc — #![strict] can't live in committed source
payload.description: CONFIRMED: `rustc --edition 2021` on a file with `#![strict]` fails with `error: cannot find attribute 'strict' in this scope`. By design — trust-lower strips it before rustc sees lowered output (crates/trust-lower/src/preprocess.rs strip_strict_attrs). Consequence: a marked file is not valid plain Rust, so it can't be committed to a repo that also builds with stock cargo. The metadata form `[package.metadata.trust] strict = true` (parsed in crates/cargo-trustc/src/main.rs:250-267) IS invisible to stock cargo and is the real answer for whole-project activation — but the README leads with #![strict]. Need: (a) promote the Cargo.toml metadata path as the primary activation; (b) offer a per-file marker that survives stock rustc (comment-form pragma, e.g. `//! trust: strict`, since register_tool is nightly-only and proc-macro inner attrs don't work on stable); (c) document the limitation honestly wherever #![strict] appears. Reviewer: this single change 'flips this from not adoptable to adopt incrementally.'
